name: stream-chat

metadata.name: stream-chat
classifiers: Topic :: Software Development :: Libraries :: Python Modules

documentation:
# Official Python SDK for [Stream Chat](https://getstream.io/chat/) [![build](https://github.com/GetStream/stream-chat-python/workflows/build/badge.svg)](https://github.com/GetStream/stream-chat-python/actions) [![PyPI version](https://badge.fury.io/py/stream-chat.svg)](http://badge.fury.io/py/stream-chat) ![PyPI - Python Version](https://img.shields.io/pypi/pyversions/stream-chat.svg) [![Checked with mypy](http://www.mypy-lang.org/static/mypy_badge.svg)](http://mypy-lang.org/)

Official Python API client for Stream Chat, a service for building chat applications.
Explore the docs »

Code Samples · Report Bug · Request Feature

--- > ### :bulb: Major update in v4.0 < > The returned response objects are instances of [`StreamResponse`](https://github.com/GetStream/stream-chat-python/blob/master/stream_chat/types/stream_response.py) class. It inherits from `dict`, so it's fully backward compatible. Additionally, it provides other benefits such as rate limit information (`resp.rate_limit()`), response headers (`resp.headers()`) or status code (`resp.status_code()`). --- ## 📝 About Stream You can sign up for a Stream account at our [Get Started](https://getstream.io/chat/get_started/) page. You can use this library to access chat API endpoints server-side. For the client-side integrations (web and mobile) have a look at the JavaScript, iOS and Android SDK libraries ([docs](https://getstream.io/chat/)). ## ⚙️ Installation ```shell $ pip install stream-chat ``` ## ✨ Getting started > :bulb: The library is almost 100% typed. Feel free to enable [mypy](https://github.com/python/mypy) for our library. We will introduce more improvements in the future in this area. ```python from stream_chat import StreamChat chat = StreamChat(api_key="STREAM_KEY", api_secret="STREAM_SECRET") # add a user chat.upsert_user({"id": "chuck", "name": "Chuck"}) # create a channel about kung-fu channel = chat.channel("messaging", "kung-fu") channel.create("chuck") # add a first message to the channel channel.send_message({"text": "AMA about kung-fu"}, "chuck") # we also expose some response metadata through a custom dictionary resp = chat.deactivate_user("bruce_lee") print(type(resp)) # print(resp["user"]["id"]) # bruce_lee rate_limit = resp.rate_limit() print(f"{rate_limit.limit} / {rate_limit.remaining} / {rate_limit.reset}") # 60 / 59 /2022-01-06 12:35:00+00:00 headers = resp.headers() print(headers) # { 'Content-Encoding': 'gzip', 'Content-Length': '33', ... } status_code = resp.status_code() print(status_code) # 200 ``` ### Async ```python import asyncio from stream_chat import StreamChatAsync async def main(): async with StreamChatAsync(api_key="STREAM_KEY", api_secret="STREAM_SECRET") as chat: # add a user await chat.upsert_user({"id": "chuck", "name": "Chuck"}) # create a channel about kung-fu channel = chat.channel("messaging", "kung-fu") await channel.create("chuck") # add a first message to the channel await channel.send_message({"text": "AMA about kung-fu"}, "chuck") # we also expose some response metadata through a custom dictionary resp = await chat.deactivate_user("bruce_lee") print(type(resp)) # print(resp["user"]["id"]) # bruce_lee rate_limit = resp.rate_limit() print(f"{rate_limit.limit} / {rate_limit.remaining} / {rate_limit.reset}") # 60 / 59 / 2022-01-06 12:35:00+00:00 headers = resp.headers() print(headers) # { 'Content-Encoding': 'gzip', 'Content-Length': '33', ... } status_code = resp.status_code() print(status_code) # 200 if __name__ == '__main__': loop = asyncio.get_event_loop() try: loop.run_until_complete(main()) finally: loop.run_until_complete(loop.shutdown_asyncgens()) loop.close() ``` ## ✍️ Contributing We welcome code changes that improve this library or fix a problem, please make sure to follow all best practices and add tests if applicable before submitting a Pull Request on Github. We are very happy to merge your code in the official repository. Make sure to sign our [Contributor License Agreement (CLA)](https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLScFKsKkAJI7mhCr7K9rEIOpqIDThrWxuvxnwUq2XkHyG154vQ/viewform) first. See our [license file](./LICENSE) for more details. Head over to [CONTRIBUTING.md](./CONTRIBUTING.md) for some development tips. ## 🧑‍💻 We are hiring! We've recently closed a [$38 million Series B funding round](https://techcrunch.com/2021/03/04/stream-raises-38m-as-its-chat-and-activity-feed-apis-power-communications-for-1b-users/) and we keep actively growing. Our APIs are used by more than a billion end-users, and you'll have a chance to make a huge impact on the product within a team of the strongest engineers all over the world. Check out our current openings and apply via [Stream's website](https://getstream.io/team/#jobs).

classes.name: stream_chat.types.segment.SegmentType
documentation:
Represents the type of segment.

Attributes:
    CHANNEL: A segment targeting channels.
    USER: A segment targeting users.

classes.name: stream_chat.types.segment.SegmentUpdatableFields
documentation:
Represents the updatable data structure for a segment.

Parameters:
    name: The name of the segment.
    description: A description of the segment.
    filter: A filter to apply to the segment.

classes.name: stream_chat.types.segment.SegmentData
documentation:
Represents the data structure for a segment.

Parameters:
    all_users: Whether to target all users.
    all_sender_channels: Whether to target all sender channels.

classes.name: stream_chat.types.base.SortOrder
documentation: Represents the sort order for a query.

classes.name: stream_chat.types.base.SortParam
documentation:
Represents a sort parameter for a query.

Parameters:
    field: The field to sort by.
    direction: The direction to sort by.

classes.name: stream_chat.types.base.Pager
documentation:
Represents the data structure for a pager.

Parameters:
    limit: The maximum number of items to return.
    next: The next page token.
    prev: The previous page token.

classes.name: stream_chat.types.stream_response.StreamResponse

methods.name: stream_chat.types.stream_response.StreamResponse.rate_limit
documentation: Returns the ratelimit info of your API operation.

methods.name: stream_chat.types.stream_response.StreamResponse.headers
documentation: Returns the headers of the response.

methods.name: stream_chat.types.stream_response.StreamResponse.status_code
documentation: Returns the HTTP status code of the response.

methods.name: stream_chat.types.stream_response.StreamResponse.is_ok
documentation: Returns True if the status code is in the 200 range.

A custom dictionary where you can access the response data the same way
as a normal dictionary. Additionally, we expose some methods to access other metadata.

```
resp = client.get_app_settings()
print(resp["app"]["webhook_url"])
# "https://mycompany.com/webhook"
rate_limit = resp.rate_limit()
print(rate_limit.remaining)
# 99
headers = resp.headers()
print(headers["content-type"])
# "application/json;charset=utf-8"
status_code = resp.status_code()
print(status_code)
# 200
```

classes.name: stream_chat.types.campaign.MessageTemplate
documentation:
Represents the data structure for a message template.

Parameters:
    text: The text of the message.
    attachments: List of the message attachments.
    custom: Custom data.

classes.name: stream_chat.types.campaign.ChannelTemplate
documentation:
Represents the data structure for a channel template.

Parameters:
    type: The type of channel.
    id: The ID of the channel.
    members: List of member IDs.
    custom: Custom data.

classes.name: stream_chat.types.campaign.CampaignData
documentation:
Represents the data structure for a campaign.

Either `segment_ids` or `user_ids` must be provided, but not both.

If `create_channels` is True, `channel_template` must be provided.

Parameters:
    message_template: The template for the message to be sent in the campaign.
    sender_id: The ID of the user who is sending the campaign.
    segment_ids: List of segment IDs the campaign is targeting.
    user_ids: List of individual user IDs the campaign is targeting.
    create_channels: Flag to indicate if new channels should be created for the campaign.
    channel_template: The template for channels to be created, if applicable.
    name: The name of the campaign.
    description: A description of the campaign.
    skip_push: Flag to indicate if push notifications should be skipped.
    skip_webhook: Flag to indicate if webhooks should be skipped.

methods.name: stream_chat.client.StreamChat.set_http_session
documentation:
You can use your own `requests.Session` instance. This instance
will be used for underlying HTTP requests.

methods.name: stream_chat.client.StreamChat._query_flag_reports
documentation:
Note: Do not use this.
It is present for internal usage only.
This function can, and will, break and/or be removed at any point in time.

methods.name: stream_chat.client.StreamChat._review_flag_report

It is present for internal usage only.

methods.name: stream_chat.async_chat.client.StreamChatAsync.set_http_session
documentation:

will be used for underlying HTTP requests.
Make sure you set up a `base_url` for the session.

methods.name: stream_chat.base.channel.ChannelInterface.send_message
documentation:
Send a message to this channel

```
the Message object

param user_id

the ID of the user that created the message

```

documentation:

```
param event

param user_id

the ID of the user sending the event

return

```

```
the id of the user

return
```

methods.name: stream_chat.base.channel.ChannelInterface.query

Query the API for this channel, get messages, members or other channel fields

```
the query options, check docs on https://getstream.io/chat/docs/
```

methods.name: stream_chat.base.channel.ChannelInterface.query_members
documentation:
Query the API for this channel to filter, sort and paginate its members efficiently.

```
param filter_conditions

filters, checks docs on https://getstream.io/chat/docs/

param sort

sorting field and direction slice, check docs on https://getstream.io/chat/docs/

param options

pagination or members based channel searching details

return

Returns members response
```

eg.
channel.query_members(filter_conditions={"name": "tommaso"},

> sort=[{"field": "created_at", "direction": -1}],
> offset=0,
> limit=10)

methods.name: stream_chat.base.channel.ChannelInterface.mark_unread
documentation:
Marks channel as unread from a specific message or thread, if thread_id is provided in data

```

param data
```

```
The server response
```

methods.name: stream_chat.base.channel.ChannelInterface.accept_invite
documentation: Accepts an invitation to this channel.

methods.name: stream_chat.base.channel.ChannelInterface.reject_invite
documentation: Rejects an invitation to this channel.

methods.name: stream_chat.base.channel.ChannelInterface.send_file
documentation:
Uploads a file.
This functionality defaults to using the Stream CDN. If you would like, you can
easily change the logic to upload to your own CDN of choice.

methods.name: stream_chat.base.channel.ChannelInterface.send_image
documentation:
Uploads an image.
Stream supported image types are: image/bmp, image/gif, image/jpeg, image/png, image/webp,
image/heic, image/heic-sequence, image/heif, image/heif-sequence, image/svg+xml.
You can set a more restrictive list for your application if needed.
The maximum file size is 100MB.

methods.name: stream_chat.base.channel.ChannelInterface.delete_file
documentation: Deletes a file by file url.

methods.name: stream_chat.base.channel.ChannelInterface.delete_image
documentation: Deletes an image by image url.

methods.name: stream_chat.base.channel.ChannelInterface.hide
documentation:
Removes a channel from query channel requests for that user until a new message is added.
Use `show` to cancel this operation.

methods.name: stream_chat.base.channel.ChannelInterface.show
documentation:
Shows a previously hidden channel.
Use `hide` to hide a channel.

methods.name: stream_chat.base.channel.ChannelInterface.mute
documentation:
Mutes a channel.
Messages added to a muted channel will not trigger push notifications, nor change the
unread count for the users that muted it. By default, mutes stay in place indefinitely
until the user removes it; however, you can optionally set an expiration time. The list
of muted channels and their expiration time is returned when the user connects.

methods.name: stream_chat.base.channel.ChannelInterface.unmute
documentation:
Unmutes a channel.
Messages added to a muted channel will not trigger push notifications, nor change the
unread count for the users that muted it. By default, mutes stay in place indefinitely
until the user removes it; however, you can optionally set an expiration time. The list
of muted channels and their expiration time is returned when the user connects.

methods.name: stream_chat.base.channel.ChannelInterface.pin
documentation:
Pins a channel
Allows a user to pin the channel (only for themselves)

methods.name: stream_chat.base.channel.ChannelInterface.unpin
documentation:
Unpins a channel
Allows a user to unpin the channel (only for themselves)

methods.name: stream_chat.base.channel.ChannelInterface.archive
documentation:
Pins a channel
Allows a user to archive the channel (only for themselves)

methods.name: stream_chat.base.channel.ChannelInterface.unarchive
documentation:
Unpins a channel
Allows a user to unpin the channel (only for themselves)

methods.name: stream_chat.base.client.StreamChatInterface.create_token
documentation:
Creates a JWT for a user.
Stream uses JWT (JSON Web Tokens) to authenticate chat users, enabling them to login.
Knowing whether a user is authorized to perform certain actions is managed
separately via a role based permissions system.
By default, user tokens are valid indefinitely. You can set an `exp`
or issued at (`iat`) claim as well.

documentation:
Verify the signature added to a webhook event

```
param request_body

the request body received from webhook

param x_signature

the x-signature header included in the request

return

bool
```

methods.name: stream_chat.base.client.StreamChatInterface.delete_user
documentation: Deletes a user synchronously. Use delete_users for batch delete.

```
param user_ids

a list of user IDs to delete

param delete_type

type of user delete (hard|soft)

param options

additional delete options

return

```

methods.name: stream_chat.base.client.StreamChatInterface.restore_users
documentation: Restores soft deleted users.

methods.name: stream_chat.base.client.StreamChatInterface.deactivate_user
documentation:
Deactivates a user.
Deactivated users cannot connect to Stream Chat, and can't send or receive messages.
To reactivate a user, use `reactivate_user` method.

methods.name: stream_chat.base.client.StreamChatInterface.reactivate_user
documentation: Reactivates a deactivated user. Use `deactivate_user` to deactivate a user.

methods.name: stream_chat.base.client.StreamChatInterface.export_user
documentation:
Exports a user. It exports a user and returns an object
containing all of it's data.

methods.name: stream_chat.base.client.StreamChatInterface.ban_user
documentation:
Bans a user. Users can be banned from an app entirely or from a channel.
When a user is banned, they will not be allowed to post messages until the
ban is removed or expired but will be able to connect to Chat and to channels as before.
To unban a user, use `unban_user` method.

methods.name: stream_chat.base.client.StreamChatInterface.shadow_ban
documentation:
Shadow ban a user.
When a user is shadow banned, they will still be allowed to post messages,
but any message sent during the will only be visible to the messages author
and invisible to other users of the App.
To remove a shadow ban, use `remove_shadow_ban` method.

methods.name: stream_chat.base.client.StreamChatInterface.remove_shadow_ban
documentation:
Removes a shadow ban of a user.
When a user is shadow banned, they will still be allowed to post messages,
but any message sent during the will only be visible to the messages author
and invisible to other users of the App.
To shadow ban a user, use `shadow_ban` method.

methods.name: stream_chat.base.client.StreamChatInterface.unban_user
documentation:
Unbans a user. Users can be banned from an app entirely or from a channel.
When a user is banned, they will not be allowed to post messages until the
ban is removed or expired but will be able to connect to Chat and to channels as before.
To ban a user, use `ban_user` method.

methods.name: stream_chat.base.client.StreamChatInterface.query_banned_users
documentation:
Queries banned users.
Banned users can be retrieved in different ways:
1) Using the dedicated query bans endpoint
2) User Search: you can add the banned:true condition to your search. Please note that
this will only return users that were banned at the app-level and not the ones
that were banned only on channels.

methods.name: stream_chat.base.client.StreamChatInterface.run_message_action
documentation: Runs a message command action.

methods.name: stream_chat.base.client.StreamChatInterface.flag_message
documentation:
Flags a message.
Any user is allowed to flag a message. This triggers the message.flagged
webhook event and adds the message to the inbox of your
Stream Dashboard Chat Moderation view.

methods.name: stream_chat.base.client.StreamChatInterface.unflag_message
documentation:
Unflags a message.
Any user is allowed to flag a message. This triggers the message.flagged
webhook event and adds the message to the inbox of your
Stream Dashboard Chat Moderation view.

methods.name: stream_chat.base.client.StreamChatInterface.query_message_flags
documentation:
Queries message flags.
If you prefer to build your own in app moderation dashboard, rather than use the Stream
dashboard, then the query message flags endpoint lets you get flagged messages. Similar
to other queries in Stream Chat, you can filter the flags using query operators.

methods.name: stream_chat.base.client.StreamChatInterface.flag_user
documentation: Flags a user.

methods.name: stream_chat.base.client.StreamChatInterface.unflag_user
documentation: Unflags a user.

methods.name: stream_chat.base.client.StreamChatInterface.mute_user
documentation:
Create a mute

```
param target_id

the user getting muted

param user_id

the user muting the target

param options

additional mute options

return

```

methods.name: stream_chat.base.client.StreamChatInterface.unmute_user
documentation:
Removes a mute

```
param target_id

the user getting un-muted

param user_id

the user muting the target

return

```

methods.name: stream_chat.base.client.StreamChatInterface.mark_all_read
documentation: Marks all messages as read for a user.

methods.name: stream_chat.base.client.StreamChatInterface.translate_message
documentation:
Translates a message

```
param message_id

Id of the message to be translated

param language

Target language of the translation

return

```

methods.name: stream_chat.base.client.StreamChatInterface.pin_message
documentation:
Pins a message.
Pinned messages allow users to highlight important messages, make announcements, or temporarily
promote content. Pinning a message is, by default, restricted to certain user roles,
but this is flexible. Each channel can have multiple pinned messages and these can be created
or updated with or without an expiration.

methods.name: stream_chat.base.client.StreamChatInterface.unpin_message
documentation:
Unpins a message.
Pinned messages allow users to highlight important messages, make announcements, or temporarily
promote content. Pinning a message is, by default, restricted to certain user roles,
but this is flexible. Each channel can have multiple pinned messages and these can be created
or updated with or without an expiration.

methods.name: stream_chat.base.client.StreamChatInterface.update_message
documentation:
Updates a message. Fully overwrites a message.
For partial update, use `update_message_partial` method.

methods.name: stream_chat.base.client.StreamChatInterface.update_message_partial
documentation:
Updates a message partially.
A partial update can be used to set and unset specific fields when
it is necessary to retain additional data fields on the object. AKA a patch style update.

methods.name: stream_chat.base.client.StreamChatInterface.delete_message
documentation: Deletes a message.

methods.name: stream_chat.base.client.StreamChatInterface.undelete_message
documentation: Undeletes a message.

methods.name: stream_chat.base.client.StreamChatInterface.get_message
documentation:
Returns a single message.
If the msg is deleted and you want to retrieve it, you can pass the show_deleted_message.

methods.name: stream_chat.base.client.StreamChatInterface.query_message_history
documentation: Queries message history.

methods.name: stream_chat.base.client.StreamChatInterface.query_users
documentation:
Allows you to search for users and see if they are online/offline.
You can filter and sort on the custom fields you've set for your user, the user id, and when the user was last active.

methods.name: stream_chat.base.client.StreamChatInterface.query_channels
documentation:
Queries channels.
You can query channels based on built-in fields as well as any custom field you add to channels.
Multiple filters can be combined using AND, OR logical operators, each filter can use its
comparison (equality, inequality, greater than, greater or equal, etc.).
You can find the complete list of supported operators in the query syntax section of the docs.

methods.name: stream_chat.base.client.StreamChatInterface.delete_channels
documentation:
Deletes multiple channels. This is an asynchronous operation and the returned value is a task Id.
You can use `get_task` method to check the status of the task.

methods.name: stream_chat.base.client.StreamChatInterface.list_commands
documentation:
Returns a list of commands.
By using custom commands, you can receive all messages sent using a specific slash command,
eg. /ticket, in your application. When configured, every slash command message happening
in a Stream Chat application will propagate to an endpoint via an HTTP POST request.

methods.name: stream_chat.base.client.StreamChatInterface.create_command
documentation:
Creates a command.
By using custom commands, you can receive all messages sent using a specific slash command,
eg. /ticket, in your application. When configured, every slash command message happening
in a Stream Chat application will propagate to an endpoint via an HTTP POST request.

By using custom commands, you can receive all messages sent using a specific slash command,

methods.name: stream_chat.base.client.StreamChatInterface.get_rate_limits
documentation:
Get rate limit quotas and usage.
If no params are toggled, all limits for all endpoints are returned.

```
param server_side

if true, show server_side limits.

param android

if true, show android limits.

param ios

if true, show ios limits.

param web

if true, show web limits.

param endpoints

restrict returned limits to the given list of endpoints.
```

methods.name: stream_chat.base.client.StreamChatInterface.search
documentation:
Searches for messages.
You can enable and/or disable the search indexing on a per channel
type through the Stream Dashboard.

methods.name: stream_chat.base.client.StreamChatInterface.send_file
documentation:
Uploads a file.
This functionality defaults to using the Stream CDN. If you would like, you can
easily change the logic to upload to your own CDN of choice.

methods.name: stream_chat.base.client.StreamChatInterface.export_channel
documentation:
Requests a channel export.
Channel exports are created asynchronously, you can use the Task ID returned by
the APIs to keep track of the status and to download the final result when it is ready.
Use `get_task` to check the status of the export.

methods.name: stream_chat.base.client.StreamChatInterface.create_import_url
documentation:
Create a URL to import a file.
Full flow:

```
url_resp = client.create_import_url("myfile.json")

upload_resp = requests.put(
    url_resp["upload_url"],
    data=open("myfile.json", "rb"),
    headers={"Content-Type": "application/json"},
)

create_resp = client.create_import(url_resp["path"], "upsert")
import_resp = client.get_import(create_resp["import_task"]["id"])
```

methods.name: stream_chat.base.client.StreamChatInterface.create_import
documentation:
Create an import task.
Full flow:

```
url_resp = client.create_import_url("myfile.json")

upload_resp = requests.put(
    url_resp["upload_url"],
    data=open("myfile.json", "rb"),
    headers={"Content-Type": "application/json"},
)

create_resp = client.create_import(url_resp["path"], "upsert")
import_resp = client.get_import(create_resp["import_task"]["id"])
```

methods.name: stream_chat.base.client.StreamChatInterface.get_import
documentation:
Get the status of an import task.
Full flow:

```
url_resp = client.create_import_url("myfile.json")

upload_resp = requests.put(
    url_resp["upload_url"],
    data=open("myfile.json", "rb"),
    headers={"Content-Type": "application/json"},
)

create_resp = client.create_import(url_resp["path"], "upsert")
import_resp = client.get_import(create_resp["import_task"]["id"])
```

methods.name: stream_chat.base.client.StreamChatInterface.list_imports
documentation:
List all import tasks. Options can contain a "limit" and "offset" parameter.
Full flow:

```
url_resp = client.create_import_url("myfile.json")

upload_resp = requests.put(
    url_resp["upload_url"],
    data=open("myfile.json", "rb"),
    headers={"Content-Type": "application/json"},
)

create_resp = client.create_import(url_resp["path"], "upsert")
import_resp = client.get_import(create_resp["import_task"]["id"])
```
